content:
Phase 3.3.1 – Verify actual screen usage of cleanup-enabled Firestore functions

Current status:
- The Cloudinary cleanup logic already exists in the Expo app
- Confirmed existing functions:
  - deleteCloudinaryImage(publicId)
  - deleteMultipleCloudinaryImages(publicIds)
  - deleteEquipmentWithCleanup(id)
  - updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup(id, updates, oldImages)
  - extractPublicIds(images)
  - getRemovedImages(oldImages, newImages)

Important constraints:
- First inspect, then only make minimal changes if needed
- Do NOT refactor
- Do NOT change unrelated UI or styling
- Do NOT change Firebase/Auth/roles
- Do NOT change the working Cloudinary upload flow
- Keep all changes incremental and localized

Your task:

1. Inspect all screens/components/actions that can:
   - delete equipment
   - edit/update equipment images
   - replace equipment images
   - save edited equipment

2. Verify whether those screens are actually calling:
   - deleteEquipmentWithCleanup()
   - updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup()

3. If they are already using the cleanup-enabled functions:
   - report that clearly
   - no code changes needed

4. If they are NOT using them:
   - make the minimum safe code changes required to switch those screens/actions to the cleanup-enabled functions
   - preserve all current UI behavior
   - do not change anything unrelated

5. Also verify whether any other delete/update path exists elsewhere in the app that still bypasses cleanup

Return:
1. Exact screens/files inspected
2. Whether cleanup is already active in real user flows
3. Exact files changed, if any
4. Short explanation of what was updated
5. Any remaining path that still bypasses Cloudinary cleanup, if any
